title: Companies Are Laying Off Workers Because of AI's Potential — Not Its Performance
excerpt: Companies are cutting jobs in anticipation of what AI might do, not what it can do today. The gap between AI hype and actual capability is driving premature workforce reductions across industries.

title: AI Was Behind Over 50,000 Layoffs in 2025 — Here Are the Top Firms to Cite It
excerpt: Amazon, Microsoft, and other tech giants cited AI as a factor in workforce reductions. At least 54,000 U.S. job cuts were directly tied to automation or artificial intelligence last year.

title: CFOs Admit Privately That AI Layoffs Will Be 9x Higher This Year
excerpt: A confidential CFO survey reveals that AI-related job cuts in 2026 could be nine times higher than 2025 levels. Companies are quietly planning massive workforce restructuring around AI adoption.

title: Investors Predict AI Is Coming for Labor in 2026
excerpt: Venture capitalists and institutional investors are betting heavily that 2026 will be the year AI meaningfully displaces human workers. The investment thesis is shifting from AI tools to AI replacements.

title: AI Is Leading to Thousands of Job Losses, Report Finds
excerpt: A new report documents thousands of jobs eliminated as companies adopt AI systems. The trend is accelerating across customer service, data entry, and content creation roles.

title: Nearly 4 in 10 Companies Will Replace Workers with AI by 2026
excerpt: A survey of 750 companies reveals that 37% expect to have replaced human jobs with AI by end of 2026. Nearly 3 in 10 say they have already done so.

title: AI Labor Displacement and the Limits of Worker Retraining
excerpt: Worker retraining programs are failing to keep pace with AI displacement. The skills gap is widening as automation advances faster than education systems can adapt.

title: How Will AI Affect the Global Workforce?
excerpt: Goldman Sachs estimates AI could automate 300 million jobs globally. Two-thirds of occupations in the U.S. and Europe are exposed to some degree of AI automation.

title: New Skills and AI Are Reshaping the Future of Work
excerpt: The IMF warns that AI will transform nearly 40% of jobs worldwide. Developing economies face even steeper challenges as automation outpaces workforce preparation.

title: How the Reskilling Revolution Will Prepare Future Workers
excerpt: The WEF projects 170 million new jobs by 2030 but 92 million displaced. Preparing 1 billion people for tomorrow's economy requires unprecedented global coordination.

title: AI and Automation Cut 54,836 U.S. Jobs in 2025
excerpt: The final tally is in: AI and automation were directly responsible for eliminating nearly 55,000 American jobs in 2025. Tech layoffs climbed 36% year over year.

title: Did AI Take Your Job? The Truth About AI Washing in Layoffs
excerpt: Some companies use AI as a convenient excuse for layoffs driven by other factors. Understanding the difference between genuine AI displacement and corporate ‘AI washing’ matters for workers.

title: Tech Layoffs 2026: How AI Is Driving the Biggest Workforce Shift
excerpt: Of 45,363 confirmed tech layoffs in early 2026, 20.4% were explicitly linked to AI. That’s a dramatic increase from 2025, when AI was cited in fewer than 8% of layoff announcements.

title: Companies That Have Replaced Workers with AI in 2025 and 2026
excerpt: A growing list of companies have publicly acknowledged replacing human workers with AI systems. From customer support to content creation, the shift is becoming undeniable.

title: Enhance or Eliminate? How AI Will Likely Change These Jobs
excerpt: New research identifies which jobs AI will enhance versus eliminate. The distinction depends on whether the work involves judgment, creativity, and human connection.

title: Retraining Workers for the Age of AI
excerpt: The U.S. workforce development system is chronically underfunded compared to peer nations. Only 4.1% of workers needing reskilling have completed AI training programs.

title: Invest in the Workforce for the AI Age: A Blueprint for Scale
excerpt: Responsible AI growth requires massive investment in human capital. The WEF outlines a blueprint for scaling skills development alongside AI adoption.

title: How Will Artificial Intelligence Affect Jobs 2026–2030
excerpt: By 2030, 92 million jobs worldwide could be replaced by AI and automation. That equals about 8% of today’s total global workforce, with mid-level roles most at risk.
